title: tisonia coriacea
intro: Tisonia coriacea, also known as the African breadnut or bush mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Scyphostegiaceae. The plant's bark, seeds, and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Tisonia coriacea include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Tisonia coriacea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, and the seeds are also used as a food source and have been shown to have antioxidant properties.
